steps.title: Identify the note that directly supports the claim
steps.content: Browner claims Ballard's compositions rely on Native musical traditions. Which note provides concrete evidence of this? Only one: Devilʼs Promenade incorporates a Seneca cow-horn rattle, a traditional Native instrument. This is the evidence the answer must use to effectively support the claim.

steps.title: Choose the answer that uses this evidence
steps.content:
Choice C is the only answer that pairs Browner's claim with the specific evidence that proves it—Devilʼs Promenade with its Seneca cow-horn rattle.

  • A uses the wrong composition (Desert Trilogy has no stated Native musical elements)
  • D includes irrelevant information about a Pulitzer Prize
  • B contains a factual error while ignoring the claim
.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
